content:
# Session 27 — Iter-017 Prover Round (Path-2 Mayer-Vietoris LES Building Blocks `HModule'_toBiprod` / `HModule'_fromBiprod`)

## Metadata

- **Archon iteration**: 017 (canonical) — Path-2 Mayer-Vietoris LES building blocks for the iter-014 / iter-016 cohomology presheaf. The first two of three LES building blocks (the third — composition-is-zero plus the connecting hom `δ` — is queued for iter-018; LES sequence + exactness for iter-019+). Refactor-then-prover phase planned; collapsed into a single prover Edit (recurrence of the iter-015 / iter-016 collapse — see "Process note").
- **Session number**: 27 (prover-round counter — sessions 22 / 23 / 24 / 25 / 26 / 27 cover iter-012 / iter-013 / iter-014 / iter-015 / iter-016 / iter-017 respectively).
- **Stage**: prover (single-lane, on `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean`).
- **Sorry count before this session**: 9 (5 `Jacobian.lean` + 3 `AbelJacobi.lean` + 1 `Picard/Functor.lean`).
- **Sorry count after this session**: 9 (unchanged at the project level — same distribution; the iter-017 plan trajectory was `9 → 11 → 9` but the refactor sub-phase did not produce separate `:= sorry` sites, so the project-level count never lifted to 11. Both new declarations were added with their closure bodies in a single Edit.).
- **Targets attempted**: 2 (`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_toBiprod`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_fromBiprod`).
- **Targets solved**: 2.
- **First-edit closure rate**: 100% (extends the streak to **eleven** consecutive prover rounds — sessions 12, 13, 14, 15, 20, 21, 22, 24, 25, 26, 27).
- **New `axiom` declarations**: 0.
- **Files edited**: 1 (`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean`).
- **Pre-processed events** (`attempts_raw.jsonl`): 17 events — 1 Edit (combined scaffold + closure of both new declarations into one `old_text`/`new_text` pair, post-iter-016 `end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme` anchor), 4 diagnostic checks (all clean — `success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []`), 2 `lean_verify` axiom checks (one per new declaration; both kernel-only), 2 `lean_run_code` runs (the per-fiber `rfl` compatibility re-probe + a `success: true` follow-up), 1 task-result write, 2 file reads, 5 incidental tool / search / shell calls. No goal-state queries (both bodies were probe-confirmed by the iter-017 plan agent and dropped in verbatim).

## Targets

### Target 1 — `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_toBiprod`

**File**: `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean`, lines 306 (docstring start) – 331 (closure body).
**Status**: SOLVED (first-edit, verbatim probe-confirmed body).
**Significance**: The first of three Mayer-Vietoris LES building blocks. A morphism `(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (op S.X₄) ⟶ (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (op S.X₂) ⊞ (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (op S.X₃)` constructed as the `biprod.lift` of the two restriction maps `(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₂₄.op` and `(...).map S.f₃₄.op`. Direct mirror of Mathlib's `GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.toBiprod` (file `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`, L43–46) for the `ModuleCat k`-flavored cohomology presheaf.

#### Goal at the would-be sorry site (post-iter-017-refactor, expected)

```lean
noncomputable def HModule'_toBiprod
    (k : Type u) [Field k]
    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
    (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₄) ⟶
      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂) ⊞
        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃) := sorry
```

The signature (frozen by `REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` §3 / PROGRESS.md "Probe-confirmed signature") declares the iter-017 carrier with the same ambient typeclass stack as iter-014 / iter-015 / iter-016 (`HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)`, `HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)`, `HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))`). `J` is implicit because it is constrained by `S : J.MayerVietorisSquare` (note the dot-notation `J.MayerVietorisSquare` resolves to `GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare J`); `S`, `F`, `n` are explicit. The codomain uses Mathlib's biproduct notation `⊞` (Mathlib `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/Shapes/Biproducts.lean`), which is auto-inferable in `AddCommGrpCat` (which has finite biproducts).

#### Attempt 1 (succeeded)

- **Strategy**: Adopt the probe-confirmed one-line term-mode body verbatim from `REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` §7 and PROGRESS.md "Probe-confirmed closure body". The body is `biprod.lift ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₂₄.op) ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₃₄.op)` — direct mirror of Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare.toBiprod` (file `MayerVietoris.lean` L43–46). The unqualified `biprod.lift` resolves under the file-level `open CategoryTheory Limits` directive (no `Limits.` prefix needed); `S.f₂₄`, `S.f₃₄`, `S.X₂`, `S.X₃`, `S.X₄` are the standard Mathlib accessors on `MayerVietorisSquare` and resolve verbatim.
- **Code tried** (`Edit` event, `attempts_raw.jsonl` log line 3, file `StructureSheafModuleK.lean`, inserted block at L306–L355 — combining the directive's scaffold and the probe-confirmed closure for *both* iter-017 declarations into one Edit):

  ```lean
  /-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-017): the first Mayer-Vietoris LES building
  block on the `ModuleCat k` side — the sum of the two restriction maps
  `(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₂₄.op` and `(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₃₄.op`,
  as a single map into the biproduct. … -/
  noncomputable def HModule'_toBiprod
      (k : Type u) [Field k]
      {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
      [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
      [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
      (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₄) ⟶
        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂) ⊞
          (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃) :=
    biprod.lift ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₂₄.op)
                ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₃₄.op)
  ```

- **Lean error**: none. `lean_diagnostic_messages` on the post-edit file (log line 5) returns `{success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []}` — zero errors, zero warnings.
- **Goal before**: term-position
  `⊢ (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₄) ⟶ (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂) ⊞ (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃)`
  under binders as in the signature.
- **Goal after**: closed.
- **Result**: success.
- **Insight**: The expression elaborates because (i) the file's `open CategoryTheory Limits` directive resolves `biprod`, `biprod.lift` with no prefix; (ii) `MayerVietorisSquare`-dot accessors `S.X₂`, `S.X₃`, `S.X₄`, `S.f₂₄`, `S.f₃₄` resolve verbatim against Mathlib's `GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare` structure; (iii) the iter-016 `HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf` is in scope under the same `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme`, so the unqualified short name resolves; (iv) `Opposite.op` qualification is required (the unqualified `op` would not resolve under `open CategoryTheory` alone — `op` lives in `Opposite`); (v) `J.MayerVietorisSquare` for `S`'s type uses dot-notation on `J : GrothendieckTopology C` to resolve the `GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare J` field — auto-inferred.

### Target 2 — `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_fromBiprod`

**File**: `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean`, lines 333 (docstring start) – 355 (closure body).
**Status**: SOLVED (first-edit, verbatim probe-confirmed body).
**Significance**: The second of three Mayer-Vietoris LES building blocks. A morphism `(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (op S.X₂) ⊞ (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (op S.X₃) ⟶ (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (op S.X₁)` constructed as the `biprod.desc` of `(...).map S.f₁₂.op` and the **negation** `-(...).map S.f₁₃.op`. Direct mirror of Mathlib's `GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.fromBiprod` (file `MayerVietoris.lean` L67–70). The negation encodes the alternating-sum convention of the Čech complex; the sign is required for the (queued iter-018) `toBiprod ≫ fromBiprod = 0` lemma.

#### Goal at the would-be sorry site (post-iter-017-refactor, expected)

```lean
noncomputable def HModule'_fromBiprod
    (k : Type u) [Field k]
    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
    (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂) ⊞
        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃) ⟶
      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₁) := sorry
```

#### Attempt 1 (succeeded)

- **Strategy**: Adopt the probe-confirmed one-line term-mode body verbatim from `REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` §7. The body is `biprod.desc ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₂.op) (-(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₃.op)` — direct mirror of Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare.fromBiprod` (file `MayerVietoris.lean` L67–70). The unary minus on the second restriction map is the standard alternating-sum convention; resolves automatically via the preadditive structure on `AddCommGrpCat`-morphisms (auto-inferred — `AddCommGrpCat` is preadditive, so morphisms in any `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` carry an `AddCommGroup` instance).
- **Code tried** (same `Edit` event as Target 1, log line 3 — both declarations land in one Edit):

  ```lean
  /-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-017): the second Mayer-Vietoris LES building
  block on the `ModuleCat k` side — the difference of the two restriction maps
  `(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₁₂.op` and `(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₁₃.op`,
  as a single map out of the biproduct. … -/
  noncomputable def HModule'_fromBiprod
      (k : Type u) [Field k]
      {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
      [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
      [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
      (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂) ⊞
          (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃) ⟶
        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₁) :=
    biprod.desc ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₂.op)
                (-(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₃.op)
  ```

- **Lean error**: none. Same clean diagnostic as Target 1 (single Edit landed both declarations).
- **Goal before**: term-position
  `⊢ (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂) ⊞ (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃) ⟶ (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₁)`
  under the same binder context.
- **Goal after**: closed.
- **Result**: success.
- **Insight**: The expression is a `biprod.desc` paired with a negated restriction map. Resolution of the unary minus relies on `AddCommGrpCat` being preadditive (auto-inferred under `open CategoryTheory`), so morphisms in `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` carry an `AddCommGroup` instance. The alternating-sum sign is what makes the queued iter-018 `toBiprod ≫ fromBiprod = 0` lemma possible (without the sign, the composition would equal `2 · (...).map S.f₁₂.op · (...).map S.f₂₄.op` rather than zero).

#### Verification (joint to both targets)

- **Compilation** (`lean_diagnostic_messages` on `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean`, log line 5): `{success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []}` — clean post-edit (one combined Edit landed both declarations, so a single post-edit diagnostic suffices for both).
- **Axioms (`HModule'_toBiprod`)** (`lean_verify` log line 6): `{axioms: ["propext", "Classical.choice", "Quot.sound"], warnings: []}` — only standard kernel axioms.
- **Axioms (`HModule'_fromBiprod`)** (`lean_verify` log line 7): `{axioms: ["propext", "Classical.choice", "Quot.sound"], warnings: []}` — only standard kernel axioms. **No new `axiom` declarations**, no `sorryAx`.
- **Per-fiber `rfl` compatibility re-probe** (`lean_run_code`, log line 12): `{success: true, diagnostics: []}` — the iter-016 compatibility relation `((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op X) : Type u) = HModule' k F n X` continues to hold by `rfl` after the iter-017 additions. This is critical: the iter-018+ `toBiprod ≫ fromBiprod = 0` lemma + LES sequence will consume this `rfl` to identify the LES carriers with the iter-014 `HModule'`.
- **Sorry count** (sorry-analyzer, log line 15): `9 total across 3 file(s)` — 5 in `Jacobian.lean`, 3 in `AbelJacobi.lean`, 1 in `Picard/Functor.lean`. **`Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` does not appear in the list** (the comment-hits in the file are not counted as active sorries). Matches the iter-017 directive's intended end-state exactly.
- **`Genus.lean`** (`lean_diagnostic_messages` log lines 9, 10, 13: clean re-checks, with one transient `success: false` at log line 13 that resolved on re-check; the `lean_run_code` smoke probe at line 12 returned `success: true`): the iter-011 honest closure of `AlgebraicGeometry.genus` remains intact.
- **`archon-protected.yaml`** (untouched): no protected declaration involved in iter-017.
- **Forbidden shortcuts**: none used. The bodies are the genuine `biprod.lift (... S.f₂₄.op) (... S.f₃₄.op)` and `biprod.desc (... S.f₁₂.op) (-(... S.f₁₃.op))` — not vacuous functors, not constant-zero placeholders, not `Iso.refl _`, not `IsZero` shortcuts on the LES diagram (LES sequence is iter-019+ work, untouched here), no `axiom`, no signature change, no protected-declaration touch, and no new `Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` file. The negation in `fromBiprod` is preserved (the prover did *not* drop the sign — that would silently break the iter-018 composition-is-zero lemma).

## File-level verification (post-prover-round)

- **Sorry count**: `9 total across 3 file(s)` — 5 in `Jacobian.lean`, 3 in `AbelJacobi.lean`, 1 in `Picard/Functor.lean`. Matches expectation.
- **`Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean`**: zero sorries, zero errors, zero warnings; ~392 LOC after this iteration (iter-016: ~341 LOC; +51 LOC for iter-017's two declarations including their docstrings). Still well under the ~400 LOC threshold flagged in PROGRESS.md / iter-016 recommendations for splitting into a separate `Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` file. The iter-006 `toModuleKSheaf` chain, iter-009 `HModule`, iter-010 `HModule_zero_linearEquiv`, iter-012 `cechCochain_OC` / `cechCohomology_OC`, iter-014 `HModule'`, iter-015 `HModule'_zero_linearEquiv`, iter-016 `HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor` / `HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf`, and iter-017 `HModule'_toBiprod` / `HModule'_fromBiprod` all coexist cleanly.
- **`Genus.lean`**: clean (iter-011 honest closure intact).
- **`archon-protected.yaml`**: untouched.
- **Forbidden shortcuts**: none used.

## Process note: refactor sub-phase collapsed into the prover Edit (third consecutive occurrence — sessions 25 + 26 + 27)

The iter-017 plan called for two sub-phases in sequence: refactor (insert the `:= sorry` scaffolds for both new declarations, taking the project-level sorry count `9 → 11`) followed by prover (replace each `sorry` with its probe-confirmed body, taking the count back to `9`). In practice — exactly as in iter-015 and iter-016 — the refactor agent did not run / did not write `task_results/refactor.md`; the prover started against the post-iter-016 file (no scaffolded `:= sorry` sites) and chose to combine the refactor's intended insertions with the closures into a single `Edit` (one `old_text` / `new_text` pair appending both directive's docstring + signature + body blocks before the closing `end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme`). The end-state matches `REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` §3 verbatim; the project-level sorry count went `9 → 9` directly, never reaching the transient 11.

This is the **third consecutive occurrence** of the same collapse (sessions 25, 26, 27), and the second consecutive paired-declaration round (after iter-016). The pattern is now firmly established for narrow probe-confirmed scaffold rounds in unprotected territory with paired-declaration shapes. PROGRESS.md (this iteration) pre-authorised the collapse, so the iter-017 occurrence is not a deviation from the directive — it is a planned compression. The substantive end-state is correct in all three sessions; only the bookkeeping lag is at issue. Worth raising with the user (still); see `recommendations.md`.

## Key findings / proof patterns (this session)

- **Eleven-consecutive-round 100% first-edit closure streak** (sessions 12, 13, 14, 15, 20, 21, 22, 24, 25, 26, 27). Probe-confirmed bodies for definitional-equality / single-Mathlib-application closures continue to land verbatim. Iter-017 is the **second** consecutive multi-target round (2 declarations), confirming the streak's generalisation to paired-declaration rounds when the second declaration is a parallel one-line term-mode wrapper around a Mathlib categorical primitive (in iter-016: `def` + `abbrev`; in iter-017: two parallel `def`s).
- **`biprod.lift` / `biprod.desc` is the canonical Mathlib idiom for the Mayer-Vietoris LES sum-of-restrictions / difference-of-restrictions building blocks** (added this session, novel). Pattern `biprod.lift (P.map f.op) (P.map g.op)` for sum-into-biproduct, `biprod.desc (P.map f.op) (-(P.map g.op))` for difference-out-of-biproduct (with the alternating-sum sign on one branch), where `P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` is any cohomology presheaf and `f, g` are the two cover legs of a Mayer-Vietoris square. Generalises across value categories with `HasFiniteBiproducts` and a `Preadditive` morphism structure (both auto-inferable in `AddCommGrpCat`). The negation is preadditive-structure-driven and does not require a separate `Neg` instance opt-in.
- **`open CategoryTheory Limits` exposes `biprod`, `biprod.lift`, `biprod.desc`** (added this session). The file's existing `open CategoryTheory Limits` directive (carried over from iter-006+) is sufficient for the iter-017 closure — no additional `open` directives needed. Contrast with iter-016 where `Abelian.extFunctor` required the `Abelian.` qualification.
- **Dot-notation on `J : GrothendieckTopology C` resolves `J.MayerVietorisSquare` to `GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare J`** (added this session). Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare` is a structure inside `GrothendieckTopology`, so `J.MayerVietorisSquare` unfolds verbatim. Avoids the more verbose `GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare J` form.
- **`MayerVietorisSquare`-dot accessors `S.X₁`, `S.X₂`, `S.X₃`, `S.X₄`, `S.f₁₂`, `S.f₁₃`, `S.f₂₄`, `S.f₃₄` resolve verbatim** (added this session). The iter-017 plan-agent's probe correctly identified these as Mathlib's standard names for the four corners and four edges of a Mayer-Vietoris square; the prover adopted them without name surgery.
- **Unary minus on a presheaf morphism is preadditive-structure-driven** (added this session). `-(P.map f.op)` for `P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` and `f : X ⟶ Y` resolves automatically via the `Preadditive` instance on `AddCommGrpCat` and the corresponding `AddCommGroup` structure on hom-sets in any functor category to a preadditive category. No explicit `Neg` instance opt-in or `AddCommGroup`-instance synthesis annotation needed in the body.
- **Per-fiber `rfl` compatibility through paired `def` + `abbrev` survives the iter-017 additions** (re-confirmed this session). The compatibility relation `((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (op X) : Type u) = HModule' k F n X` continues to hold by `rfl` after the iter-017 additions (the iter-016 abbrev wrapping is unchanged); the iter-017 declarations consume this relation indirectly (their domain/codomain are `(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (op S.Xᵢ)` for various `i`, which by `rfl` are `HModule' k F n S.Xᵢ`).
- **Refactor + prover sub-phase collapse for narrow probe-confirmed scaffolds is now a *recurring three-times-in-a-row* execution mode** (sessions 25, 26, 27). When the directive's scaffolds + closures are all single-block changes in unprotected territory, a single prover Edit (one `old_text` / `new_text` pair) can satisfy both phases for both declarations. Cost: missing `task_results/refactor.md` bookkeeping. Since iter-015 / iter-016 / iter-017 PROGRESS.md all pre-authorised the collapse, the dispatcher / plan agent should consider standardising this for future narrow probe-confirmed rounds.
- **Plan-agent `lean_run_code` probe correctness rate is 100% on eleven consecutive rounds** (sessions 12, 13, 14, 15, 20, 21, 22, 24, 25, 26, 27). For narrow-scope rounds with single-Mathlib-call closures (this iteration: two parallel `biprod.lift` / `biprod.desc` term-mode bodies with negation on one branch), "adopt and verify" with no mid-edit budget is correct.

## Recommendations for next session

See `recommendations.md` for the actionable briefing for the iter-018 plan agent. Headline: with the first two LES building blocks (`HModule'_toBiprod`, `HModule'_fromBiprod`) now landed, the leading priority for iter-018 is **the composition-is-zero lemma `toBiprod ≫ fromBiprod = 0` and the connecting hom `δ`** (the third Mayer-Vietoris LES building block). Iter-019+ then assembles the LES sequence + iso to `Ext.contravariantSequence` + exactness theorem. The 8 remaining protected sorries plus the deferred `representable` are all gated behind multi-iteration upstream work; do not assign as direct prover objectives.

## Blueprint markers updated

- `Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`, `def:Scheme_HModule_prime_toBiprod` (definition block, ≈L519–L537): added `\leanok` to the statement block — the Lean declaration `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_toBiprod` exists at L321–L331 of `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` with the directive's signature and the file compiles cleanly (verified `lean_diagnostic_messages` `success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []`).
- `Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`, proof block of `def:Scheme_HModule_prime_toBiprod` (≈L539–L543): added `\leanok` — the body is the honest one-liner `biprod.lift ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₂₄.op) ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₃₄.op)` (no `sorry`), kernel-only axioms (`propext`, `Classical.choice`, `Quot.sound`).
- `Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`, `def:Scheme_HModule_prime_fromBiprod` (definition block, ≈L547–L565): added `\leanok` to the statement block — the Lean declaration `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_fromBiprod` exists at L345–L355 with the directive's signature and the file compiles cleanly.
- `Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`, proof block of `def:Scheme_HModule_prime_fromBiprod` (≈L567–L571): added `\leanok` — the body is the honest one-liner `biprod.desc ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₂.op) (-(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₃.op)` (no `sorry`), kernel-only axioms.
- The `Why these are the iter-017 targets` and `Compatibility with Mathlib's AddCommGrpCat-flavored LES` blocks (≈L573–L583) are `\begin{remark}` blocks; per the marker vocabulary in `CLAUDE.md`, `\leanok` is reserved for theorems / lemmas / definitions, not remarks. No marker added; the sub-step's substantive verification is captured in the proof-block markers above and in this summary.
